title: carex maritima subsp. lyngbyei
intro: Carex maritima subsp. lyngbyei, also known as Lyngbye's sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that is native to the Arctic tundra and coastal areas of northern Europe, Greenland, and northern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex maritima subsp. lyngbyei include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infections, making it a valuable resource for individuals living in areas with limited access to medical care. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional remedies for skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
